summary: Sidebar width can be made to exceed normal ranges

description.text: ### Description It is possible to get the sidebar width values in the `config.json` to get set to a value outside of the normal maximum or minimum widths by having developer mode enabled. The same is possible with the filetree bar on the right side, however the results are not nearly as broken as the sidebar. The end result of this is a sidebar that may either not function correctly, or entirely breaks the workspace, rendering the entire editor inoperable while the sidebar is open. In extreme cases, this can require manual modification of the configuration in `config.json` back to normal values. This also can cause text-rendering failures in the editor. This seems to be a side effect related to Javascript, as shown in attached images. ### Reproducing This can be reproduced with some consistency by doing the following steps. 1. Set the **filetree bar** to it's maximum possible width with Zettlr maximised. 2. Set the **sidebar** to it's minimum possible width with Zettlr maximised. 3. Open the developer console, and make sure it is on the **right side** and then enable **toggle device toolbar** 4. Make the dev console as large as you can, and or resize Zettlr's window itself. 5. The sidebar will start to be set to unusual sizes, and this value is saved to `config.json`. This can break the sidebar in exceptional cases, requiring manual reconfiguration. ### Zettlr version Zettlr 3.6.0 ### Installation Method Flathub ### Installation Method (Other) _No response_ ### Your Platform - [ ] Windows - [ ] macOS - [x] Linux ### Architecture - [x] Intel 64bit - [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon) ### Operating System Version Linux Mint Debian Edition ### Additional Information This is not likely to happen under most normal usage of Zettlr, and only concerns those who regularly use the developer console, and especially those who resize it or the sidebar. This was first noticed with less extreme conditions than the reproduction steps, therefor it may be possible it can break under other various conditions as-well. NOTE: Attached files are not using a **clean, and vanilla** CSS. Importantly, fonts were modified, and so was the titlebar and toolbar at the time of taking these screenshots. This was tested without custom CSS to the same results. **Screenshot of config value shown in KATE.** **Showing the editor rendered inoperable by both values being 0.** **Text Rendering stops working in the editor with these errors in the devconsole at the same time.**

summary: Citations are replaced by their rendered versions on overwriting selections

description.text: ### Description I just found a very curious bug in the current nightly release while writing my background section. If I select a part of a paragraph which I wish to delete, and then start typing, Zettlr will replace all citations after the selection end within that paragraph with their rendered counterparts. This does not happen if I delete the section first, and only then start typing (i.e., not "overwriting" the selection). ### Reproducing Before (includes the selection): After (typing a single space, which replaces the selection): Note how the two citations at the end of the paragraph suddenly turn into their rendered counterparts. Indeed, it is literal text, and not a simple visual bug. ### Zettlr version 3.6.0-nightly (August 2026) ### Installation Method From the Website or GitHub ### Installation Method (Other) _No response_ ### Your Platform - [ ] Windows - [x] macOS - [ ] Linux ### Architecture - [ ] Intel 64bit - [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon) ### Operating System Version macOS Sonoma 15.6 ### Additional Information I have absolutely no idea what is going on here.

summary: Table Editor: add keybindings (Up and Down arrow keys, and Tab to create new row)

description.text:
### Description

Add keybindings for the table editor to use the Up and Down arrows to move between the cells, and Tab in the last cell of the table to add an additional row.



### Proposed Changes

It would be handy to add keybindings (1) for the Up and Down arrows to move between the cells, and (2) Tab in the last cell of the table to add an additional row.

Implementing these would bring Zettlr in line with how the keybindings work in Word and LibreOffice.

### Caveats

None

summary: Error in snippets with Spanish caracters

description.text:
### Description

When you use snippets and a word is copied in any other part of the text, the Spanish accents are not properly written in the copy.

### Reproducing

Use a snippet with 2 or more variables. For example:

  ---
  title: " ${1:Enter a Title}"
  ---
  
  #   ${1:Enter a Title}

When you write an accent in the first ${1:Enter a Title} appears as it should, but in the copy (the #   ${1:Enter a Title}) just appears the accent but not the letter. For example: I write "día" on metadata and appears "d'a" on title.

### Zettlr version

3.6.0.

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

Ubuntu terminal, using .deb file.

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Ubuntu

### Additional Information

Many thanks for the help!

summary: Uncollapsing folders causes similarly named folders to uncollapse as well

description.text:
### Description

I have two folders, `Applications` and `Applications_Postdoc` in the same folder. Whenever I do something that triggers an uncollapsing (function: `uncollapseIfApplicable`) on the latter folder, the former will also uncollapse, which is undesirable.

### Reproducing

Have two folders in the same parent that start with the same name, where one of them has simply some addition afterwards. While trying to uncollapse the latter, the former will also uncollapse.

### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

macOS, but this bug is platform-independent

### Additional Information

The bug is caused by this part of the function `uncollapseIfApplicable`:

https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/blob/fd12664d6faacef4c1a513e9ce912eec99aaffae/source/win-main/file-manager/TreeItem.vue#L437-L442

Essentially, it just checks whether the current path starts with the provided name, which returns true for the folder `path/to/folder` if the path name is `path/to/folder_suffix`.

summary: Allow to move/drag Zettlr when not using window titles

description.text: ### Checklist - [ ] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.) - [x] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.) - [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it. - [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this. ### Feature Proposal Maybe a quite particular request, but for those like me using a tiling window manager (I use Pop!_OS) it's impossible to move Zettlr window if the window title is hidden. A window with a title looks like this_ And I can drag it normally with the mouse if I click anywhere in the title. I'd like to be able to move the window by clicking anywhere in the top of the application: ### Example Workflow Anytime I want to move the window, it's impossible as the window title is hidden. ### Caveats None. ### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself? No ### Platforms - [ ] Windows - [ ] macOS - [x] Linux ### Additional Information Pop!_OS 22.04 LTS x86_64 with GNOME 42.9 and Mutter (X11)

summary: Typewriter mode does not center vertically

description.text:
### Description

Hey Zettlr team,

I’m on version 3.5.1 and I love using Typewriter Mode, but I just discovered that it no longer keeps my current line in the middle of the screen. In older versions, as soon as I hit Ctrl + Alt + T, my line would stay locked right in the center while I typed or moved around. Now as soon as I write more than a few words or scroll with the arrow keys, the line drifts off toward the top or bottom and I lose that nice typewriter feel.

**How to see the issue yourself**

1. Open Zettlr 3.5.1 on Windows, macOS, or Linux.
2. Load any markdown file.
3. Turn on Typewriter Mode (View → Typewriter Mode or Ctrl + Alt + T).
4. Type continuously or press the down arrow until you hit the bottom of the window.

You’ll notice that instead of staying centered, the text scrolls normally. It really breaks the flow of writing when the line moves out of view. I checked issue #2682 and saw it got closed, but the behavior still feels like a regression from the old v1.x experience.

**What I’d like to see**
Please bring back the centering logic that pins the cursor line to the middle of the editor view. Even if you expose it as an optional setting, that would be amazing.

Thanks for all your hard work on Zettlr—I hope this can get fixed soon!


### Reproducing

Here’s a detailed, step‑by‑step recipe to reproduce the issue on your side. Feel free to adapt any pathnames or filenames to your system:

1. **Prepare your environment**

   * Install or upgrade to **Zettlr 3.5.1** on your platform (Windows 10/11, macOS 12+, or a modern Linux distro).
   * Make sure **no Custom CSS** is applied for editor theming (i.e. reset any CSS in Assets Manager → Custom CSS).

2. **Create or open a sample document**

   * Create a new Markdown file (e.g. `test.md`) with at least 50 lines of text. For example, paste a few paragraphs of Lorem Ipsum so you have room to scroll.

3. **Launch Zettlr and load your file**

   * Open Zettlr, then open `test.md` via **File → Open** (or drag‑and‑drop into the editor).

4. **Enable Typewriter Mode**

   * Go to **View → Typewriter Mode**, or press **Ctrl+Alt+T** (⌘+Alt+T on macOS).
   * Verify that “Typewriter Mode” is highlighted in the View menu.

5. **Position your cursor mid‑document**

   * Click anywhere around line 25 so that your cursor (point) is roughly in the middle of the file.
   * Confirm that before typing, the line is initially centered in the editor viewport.

6. **Trigger scrolling by typing**

   * Begin typing on that line until you’ve exceeded the bottom half of the viewport (e.g. add text until the cursor would naturally scroll).
   * Observe that instead of staying fixed in the vertical center, the cursor line moves toward the bottom of the window and eventually disappears from center.

7. **Trigger scrolling by navigation**

   * Place the cursor near the top of the document, then repeatedly press the **Down Arrow** until you pass the halfway point of the viewport.
   * Again, note that the active line drifts instead of remaining locked at center.

8. **Try with both themes**

   * Switch between **Light** and **Dark** themes (Settings → Theme) to confirm the behavior is identical in each.

9. **Capture screen recording or logs**

   * (Optional but helpful) Record a short screencast of the editor window while you type/arrow‑scroll.
   * If available, collect Zettlr’s console logs (Help → Toggle Developer Tools → Console) to see any errors.

—
With these exact steps, you should see the active line “unstick” from the middle as soon as scrolling occurs. Let me know if you need more details or a repro video!


### Zettlr version

3.5.1

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

summary: Memory bug - Insert Image from Clipboard

description.text:
### Description

Been working in a markdown file for some time while adding images which to take notes.
Some images weren't clear so I removed them and replaced them.

After a few hours of work Zettlr memory consumption was over 2GB of ram in a document containing roughly 2000 words, a bit of LateX and 30 images.

After restarting the application, Zettlr was significantly faster & memory consumption was way down for the same file (scrolled through whole document to see if it buffered images into memory but that only got me to 300MB of consumption).

### Reproducing

Easiest way to test:

1. Take a screenshot (must be new and not added before)
2. Copy from clipboard into Zettlr
3. Add it, remove the markdown from the added image
4. Repeat steps 1-3 and say goodbye to your memory (after a while)

### Zettlr version

3.5.1

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

summary: Can't Add Words to Spelling Dictionary

description.text:
### Description

When I click on a word that is falsely indicated as being misspelled, and attempt to add it to the Zettlr spellchecker, I am not given the option to add it to the dictionary. 

### Reproducing

- In a body of text, find a word that is incorrectly noted as being misspelled.
- Click on the word that is falsely noted as misspelled.
- An option comes up indicating the word is misspelled even though it is not.
- But no option is given to add the word to the Zettlr dictionary.

### Zettlr version

Version 3.5.1

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

15.5 (24F74)

### Additional Information

Note: I believe this is a little different than another reported issue. As I read that bug report it seemed that the author was allowed to add a word to the dictionary, but the dictionary later deleted the word. In my case, I am not even given the option to add the word to the dictionary.

summary: Can't create New Directory on Linux Mint

description.text: ### Description I installed Zettlr but can't add new directory. When I click it, nothing happens. Tried various different solutions from chatGPT like: Opening it from terminal, Forcing it to open with x11 Installing different versions (Ubuntu, AppImage, Flatpack) few others At this point even ChatGPT started to suggest me installing different app, but other apps don't seem that versatile and user-friendly. And I don't want to pay for Obsidian. Anyone know what can be wrong? -- ### Reproducing Click on new directory and nothing happens ### Zettlr version 3.5.1 ### Installation Method From the Website or GitHub ### Installation Method (Other) _No response_ ### Your Platform - [ ] Windows - [ ] macOS - [x] Linux ### Architecture - [ ] Intel 64bit - [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon) ### Operating System Version Linux Mint ### Additional Information _No response_
